pattern: Binary searching on the value and counting, row by row, how many table entries do not exceed a candidate finds the k-th smallest without materializing the table.
hints: The answer is some integer between one and m times n; you do not need to build the whole table.
For a candidate value, row i contributes the count of multiples of i that stay within both the value and n.
Binary search the value: find the smallest value whose count of entries at most that value reaches k.

edge_inputs.description: single row reduces to k itself

stress.description: random dimensions with k within one to m times n
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, min(max(size, 1), 60))
    n = rng.randint(1, min(max(size, 1), 60))
    k = rng.randint(1, m * n)
    return [m, n, k]
